verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A encodes the inducible cytosolic HSP70 chaperone. Its canonical localization is cytoplasmic/nuclear, but robust literature documents stress-induced and tumor-cell translocation to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane (cell-surface HSP70 / csHSP70), where it acts as a recognition signal for NK cells and serves as a target for antibody, CAR-T, and tumor-vaccine programs. This cancer/stress-induced ecto-display qualifies as contextual / cell_state_induced surface accessibility. The protein lacks its own TM domain or GPI anchor; surface display is dependent on cell stress or oncogenic transformation.
